sections.title: Performance Analysis: Axis CLI (Go)
sections.content:
**Note:** This is a Go CLI application with no React components. The "re-renders" finding is N/A.

---

sections.title: 1. N+1 Query Patterns
2. Caching Opportunities
sections.content:
**A. `Registry.List()` allocates on every call** (tool.go:62-70)
```go
func (r *Registry) List() []types.ToolDefinition {
    r.mu.RLock()
    defer r.mu.RUnlock()
    defs := make([]types.ToolDefinition, 0, len(r.tools))  // ALLOCATES every call!
    for _, t := range r.tools {
        defs = append(defs, t.Schema())  // Also calls Schema() repeatedly
    }
    return defs
}
```

**Fix:** Cache the combined schema slice with invalidation on Register:
```go
type Registry struct {
    mu           sync.RWMutex
    tools        map[string]Tool
    scopes       map[string][]string
    cachedDefs   []types.ToolDefinition  // invalidate on Register/Delete
    cacheValid   bool
}
```

**B. `MemoryStateStore` has no read-through cache** - every Load hits the map + mutex.

**C. `parseSLA` is called per-task execution** (orchestrator.go:170) - consider caching parsed SLA:
```go
// Current: called on every executeTask
timeoutMs, maxRetries, failureClass, backoff := parseSLA(task.Metadata)
```

---

sections.title: 3. Memory Leaks
4. Redundant Computations
sections.content:
**A. `time.Now()` called 3x per state save** (scheduler.go:294-322)
```go
now := time.Now()
switch status {
case types.TaskStatusRunning:
    task.StartedAt = &now
case types.TaskStatusCompleted, types.TaskStatusFailed:
    task.CompletedAt = &now
}
state := types.TaskState{
    Task:      task,
    UpdatedAt: time.Now(),  // Called again!
}
```

**Fix:** Reuse the same `now` value.

**B. `sort.SliceStable` allocates closure** (scheduler.go:184-186)
```go
sort.SliceStable(candidates, func(i, j int) bool {
    return getPriority(candidates[i]) > getPriority(candidates[j])
})
```
`getPriority` parses metadata string → int for each comparison. With 100 tasks, this is O(n log n) string conversions.

**Fix:** Pre-compute priorities during first pass:
```go
for _, task := range s.queue {
    if task.Status == types.TaskStatusPending && s.areDependenciesCompleted(task.Dependencies) {
        task.priority = getPriority(task)  // Cache it
        candidates = append(candidates, task)
    }
}
```

**C. `orchestrator.runTaskLoop` polls with 100ms sleep** when empty (line 151):
```go
case <-time.After(100 * time.Millisecond):
```
Consider using `taskSubmitted` channel more efficiently - it already signals on new task.

---

sections.title: 5. Lock Contention
sections.content:
**A. `GetAllTasks` takes a full read lock** (scheduler.go:247) and does deep copies:
```go
func (s *SchedulerImpl) GetAllTasks() []*types.AgentTask {
    s.mu.RLock()
    defer s.mu.RUnlock()
    // ... deep copies of every field
}
```
If called frequently (e.g., for status polling), this serializes all workers.

**B. Multiple sequential locks in `executeTask`** (orchestrator.go:169-187):
1. `scheduler.GetStatus` → RLock
2. `scheduler.UpdateTaskStatus` → Lock

Could combine into single lock if called together.

---

sections.title: Summary Table
sections.content:
| Issue | Severity | Impact |
|-------|----------|--------|
| N+1 dependency lookups | Medium | O(n×d) → O(n+d) |
| Registry.List() allocation | Low | Per-call GC pressure |
| Goroutine leak potential | Medium | Resource exhaustion under cancellation |
| Redundant time.Now() calls | Low | Minor CPU waste |
| sort.SliceStable with string parsing | Medium | O(n log n) → O(n) |
| 100ms polling interval | Low | Latency for new tasks |

The N+1 dependency lookup in `GetReadyTasks` is the highest-impact finding.

codeBlocks.code:
type Registry struct {
    mu           sync.RWMutex
    tools        map[string]Tool
    scopes       map[string][]string
    cachedDefs   []types.ToolDefinition  // invalidate on Register/Delete
    cacheValid   bool
}

// Current: called on every executeTask
timeoutMs, maxRetries, failureClass, backoff := parseSLA(task.Metadata)

var history []types.ModelMessage  // Appends every turn, never trimmed
maxTurns := 10
